sections.title: Part 2
sections.passage: You will hear a lifeboat volunteer called David talking about his work.

questionGroups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: What made David leave London and move to Northsea?

options.text: He was eager to develop a hobby.
He wanted to work shorter hours.
He found his job in website design unsatisfying.

questions.question: The Lifeboat Institution in Northsea was built with money provided by

options.text: a local organisation.
a local resident.
the local council.

questions.question: In his health assessment, the doctor was concerned about the fact that David

options.text: might be colour blind.
was rather short-sighted.
had undergone eye surgery.

questions.question: After arriving at the lifeboat station, they aim to launch the boat within

options.text: five minutes.
six to eight minutes.
eight and a half minutes.

questions.question: As a 'helmsman', David has the responsibility of deciding

options.text: who will be the members of his crew.
what equipment it will be necessary to take.
if the lifeboat should be launched.

questions.question: As well as going out on the lifeboat, David

options.text: gives talks on safety at sea.
helps with fundraising.
recruits new volunteers.

questionGroups.question: Which TWO things does David say about the lifeboat volunteer training?

options.text: The residential course developed his leadership skills.
The training in use of ropes and knots was quite brief.
The training exercises have built up his mental strength.
The casualty care activities were particularly challenging for him.
The wave tank activities provided practice in survival techniques.

questionGroups.question: Which TWO things does David find most motivating about the work he does?

options.text: working as part of a team
experiences when working in winter
being thanked by those he has helped
the fact that it keeps him fit
the chance to develop new equipment

sections.title: Part 3
sections.passage: You will hear two students, Don and Bella, discussing their project on recycling footwear.

questionGroups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: At first, Don thought the topic of recycling footwear might be too

options.text: limited in scope.
hard to research.
boring for listeners.

questions.question: When discussing trainers, Bella and Don disagree about

options.text: how popular they are among young people.
how suitable they are for school.
how quickly they wear out.

questions.question: Bella says that she sometimes recycles shoes because

options.text: they no longer fit.
she no longer likes them.
they are no longer in fashion.

questions.question: What did the article say that confused Don?

options.text: Public consumption of footwear has risen.
Less footwear is recycled now than in the past.
People dispose of more footwear than they used to.

questionGroups.instructions: What reasons did the recycling manager give for rejecting footwear, according to the students? Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–F, next to Questions 25–28.

options.text: one shoe was missing
the colour of one shoe had faded
one shoe had a hole in it
the shoes were brand new
the shoes were too dirty
the stitching on the shoes was broken

questionGroups.instructions: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.question: Why did the project to make 'new' shoes out of old shoes fail?

options.text: People believed the 'new' pairs of shoes were unhygienic.
There were not enough good parts to use in the old shoes.
The shoes in the 'new' pairs were not completely alike.

questions.question: Bella and Don agree that they can present their topic

options.text: from a new angle.
with relevant images.
in a straightforward way.

sections.title: Part 4
sections.passage: You will hear a lecture about tardigrades, also known as water bears.

questionGroups.title: Tardigrades

content.text:
● more than 1,000 species, 0.05–1.2 millimetres long
● also known as water 'bears' (due to how they

content.text:
round body and four pairs of legs

for gripping

and blood

with teeth called stylets

ensures their DNA is not damaged.
